name: Leo Martinez
text: The parser is a lifesaver for studio deadlines. It caught every critique date perfectly, and the flashcard maker is a lifesaver with my high-res blueprints.

name: Sarah Chen
text: CourseQ turned my 60 page pharmacology syllabus into a manageable weekly plan. The calendar sync is seamless, making my clinical rotations much easier to track.

name: Marcus Thorne
text: The parsing logic is great. It scraped my Data Structures syllabus in under a minute. I love having the study timer integrated into the same app.

name: Aaliyah Jones
text: Dragging PDFs into CourseQ feels like magic. It pulled every reading assignment and created great flashcards for case law, with only minor edits needed.

name: Julian Voss
text: The weekly plan is so intuitive. It rescheduled my reviews automatically when I was away for field research. It’s nearly perfect for a busy lab schedule.

name: Elena Rodriguez
text: The Google Calendar sync is a total game changer. The app handles the heavy lifting of mapping out my week, and the flashcards support my complex math formulas.

name: Samir Gupta
text: Reliability is key, and CourseQ hasn't missed a deadline yet. It finds perfect gaps in my schedule for focus sessions without me having to overthink it.

name: Chloe Bennett
text: Finally, an app with a beautiful UI! It makes tracking dates feel less like a chore. The study timer is a nice touch for staying focused with my roommate.

name: Liam O'Shea
text: Scanning long seminar syllabi used to take hours. CourseQ pulled every essay prompt into my calendar in seconds. It’s brilliant for memorizing dates and names.

name: Maya Patel
text: I’m obsessed with how the weekly plan tells me exactly when to start reviewing metabolic pathways. The iCloud sync is flawless and keeps my lab days super organized.

name: David Kim
text: The focus timer keeps me off social media, and I love how the app gamifies the weekly plan. It’s a very thoughtful system for managing a heavy course load.

name: Olivia Wright
text: It caught every single 'pitch due' date in my syllabus. I use the flashcards for AP Style rules, and the weekly plan keeps me on track with my long course features.
